content.problemStatement:
**This is the easy version of the problem. Here, it is guaranteed that P2i=0P_{2i} = 0P2i‚Äč=0 for all iii.**

You are given a permutation‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† PPP of the integers 111 to 2N2N2N, with some of its elements missing (represented by 000's).

Find the number of ways of filling in the zeros to obtain a valid permutation, such that the quantity

‚ą£P1‚ąíP2‚ą£+‚ą£P3‚ąíP4‚ą£+‚Ä¶+‚ą£P2N‚ąí1‚ąíP2N‚ą£|P_1 - P_2| + |P_3 - P_4| + \ldots + |P_{2N-1} - P_{2N}|‚ą£P1‚Äč‚ąíP2‚Äč‚ą£+‚ą£P3‚Äč‚ąíP4‚Äč‚ą£+‚Ä¶+‚ą£P2N‚ąí1‚Äč‚ąíP2N‚Äč‚ą£is maximized.

**In this version of the problem, there is an additional constraint on the input:** all the elements at even positions are 000, that is,

P2=P4=P6=‚Ä¶=P2N=0P_2 = P_4 = P_6 = \ldots = P_{2N} = 0P2‚Äč=P4‚Äč=P6‚Äč=‚Ä¶=P2N‚Äč=0.

Note that the elements at odd positions may or may not be zero.

The answer can be large, so find it modulo 998244353998 244 353998244353.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† A permutation of the integers 111 to NNN is an array of length NNN that contains every integer from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN ‚ÄĒ meaning that PPP has length 2N2N2N.
The second line contains 2N2N2N space-separated integers P1,P2,‚Ä¶,P2NP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_{2N}P1‚Äč,P2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,P2N‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of ways of filling in the zeros to maximize the sum, modulo 998244353998 244 353998244353.
constraints: All the non-zero elements of PPP are distinct.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚čÖ1052\cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: There are 666 permutations we can form by filling in the zeros:

[4,3,1,5,2,6][4, 3, 1, 5, 2, 6][4,3,1,5,2,6] with a value of ‚ą£4‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí6‚ą£=9|4-3| + |1-5| + |2-6| = 9‚ą£4‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí6‚ą£=9.
[4,3,1,6,2,5][4, 3, 1, 6, 2, 5][4,3,1,6,2,5] with a value of ‚ą£4‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí6‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí5‚ą£=9|4-3| + |1-6| + |2-5| = 9‚ą£4‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí6‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí5‚ą£=9.
[4,5,1,3,2,6][4, 5, 1, 3, 2, 6][4,5,1,3,2,6] with a value of ‚ą£4‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí6‚ą£=7|4-5| + |1-3| + |2-6| = 7‚ą£4‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí6‚ą£=7.
[4,5,1,6,2,3][4, 5, 1, 6, 2, 3][4,5,1,6,2,3] with a value of ‚ą£4‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí6‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí3‚ą£=7|4-5| + |1-6| + |2-3| = 7‚ą£4‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí6‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí3‚ą£=7.
[4,6,1,3,2,5][4, 6, 1, 3, 2, 5][4,6,1,3,2,5] with a value of ‚ą£4‚ąí6‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí5‚ą£=7|4-6| + |1-3| + |2-5| = 7‚ą£4‚ąí6‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí3‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí5‚ą£=7.
[4,6,1,5,2,3][4, 6, 1, 5, 2, 3][4,6,1,5,2,3] with a value of ‚ą£4‚ąí6‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí3‚ą£=7|4-6| + |1-5| + |2-3| = 7‚ą£4‚ąí6‚ą£+‚ą£1‚ąí5‚ą£+‚ą£2‚ąí3‚ą£=7.

The maximum possible value is 999, and two permutations attain that maximum.
Test case 222: The maximum possible value is 161616, attained by 242424 permutations.
Test case 333: There are 242424 ways of filling in the zeros to obtain a permutation; of which it can be verified that 161616 of them attain the maximum possible value of 444.
